God was about to deprive Judah of every support. The city and the land were to become desolate because their words and actions had been rebellious against the Lord, even at his holy temple. If people do not rely on God, he will quickly remove all other supports, and then they must fall. Christ is the Bread of life and the Water of life; if he is our support, we will find that it is a good thing that cannot be taken away. Here note, 1. That the condition of sinners is exceedingly sad. 2. It is the soul that is damaged by sin. 3. Whatever evil happens to sinners, they bring it on themselves.

The rule was certain; whether there might be national prosperity or trouble, it would be well with the righteous and ill with the wicked. Blessed be God, there is abundant encouragement for the righteous to trust in him, and for sinners to repent and return to him. It was time for the Lord to show his power. He will call people to account for all the wealth and power entrusted to and abused by them. If it is sinful to disregard the necessities of the poor, how odious and wicked are those who bring people into poverty, and then oppress them!

The prophet reproves and warns the daughters of Zion about the suffering coming upon them. Let them know that God notices the folly and vanity of proud women, even of their dress. The punishments threatened answered the sin. Loathsome diseases often are a just punishment for pride. It is not important to ask what sort of ornaments they wore; many of these things would have been ridiculed then as now if they had not been in fashion. Their fashions differed much from those of our times, but human nature is the same. Wasting time and money, to the neglect of piety, charity, and even justice, displease the Lord. Many professors today seem to think there is no harm in worldly finery; but would the Holy Spirit have taught the prophet to expose it so fully if it were not a great evil? The Jews being overcome, Jerusalem would be leveled with the ground; which is represented under the idea of a desolate female seated upon the earth. And when the Romans had destroyed Jerusalem, they struck a medal, on which was represented a woman sitting on the ground in a posture of grief. If sin is harbored within the walls, lamentation and mourning are near the gates.
